item.title: Too early to judge Seruvakula after just two Tests - Rabuka
item.description: Too early to judge Seruvakula after just two Tests - Rabuka

item.title: All Blacks recruit Rieko Ioane as injury cover, Scott Barrett ruled out of South Africa tour

item.title: Discipline and respect for referees' key to Cakaudrove's Ranadi title bid
item.description: Cakaudrove captain Finau Kaulele says discipline and respect for referees will be key as the team sets its sights on winning this year's Ranadi Cup after falling short in recent campaigns. The 23-year-old from Naweni Village in Savusavu says the team has made discipline one of its top priorities and continues to remind players to listen to and respect the referee throughout every match.
item.content:encoded:
Cakaudrove captain Finau Kaulele says discipline and respect for referees will be key as the team sets its sights on winning this year's Ranadi Cup after falling short in recent campaigns.

The 23-year-old from Naweni Village in Savusavu says the team has made discipline one of its top priorities and continues to remind players to listen to and respect the referee throughout every match.

Insert: VITI-Captain on discipline 13th July 26

Kaulele, who has been playing for Cakaudrove for the past three years, says every player is expected to follow the laws of the game to ensure matches are played in a fair and sporting manner.

She says new players are especially encouraged never to argue with referees or question their decisions on the field.

Kaulele says Cakaudrove's main goal this year is to reach the final and win the Raluve Tournament.

She says the team reached the final in 2024, where they lost to Kadavu, before making the quarter finals in last year's competition.

Kaulele also highlighted the importance of community support, saying the team has traditionally relied on fundraising to meet its expenses.

She says this year has been different, with Loloma Production providing playing vests, clothing, pants and other assistance, easing some of the financial burden on the players.

Kaulele thanked the team's sponsor, coach and players for their continued commitment and support as they prepare for this year's tournament.

Meanwhile, Cakaudrove will face Bua at 11am tomorrow at Ratu Ganilau Park.

In other matches tomorrow, Tavua will play Yasawa at Theodore Park, Northern Bulls will take on Northland at Ratu Cakobau Park, Ra will meet Coastland at Narauyaba Sports Ground, Malolo will take on Navosa at Lawaqa Park and Ovalau will face Serua at Nasau Park.

The Skipper Cup and the Subrail Marama Championship will kick off next month.

item.title: Drua Women ready to rise for Reds semi-final showdown at Churchill Park this Saturday
item.description: The McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women are embracing the challenge ahead as they prepare for a high-stakes Super Rugby Women semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, with captain Bitila Tawake saying the team is determined to keep improving and earn a place in the grand final. The Drua will face the Reds at Churchill Park this Saturday, knowing they will be up against a side full of confidence
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women are embracing the challenge ahead as they prepare for a high-stakes Super Rugby Women semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, with captain Bitila Tawake saying the team is determined to keep improving and earn a place in the grand final.

The Drua will face the Reds at Churchill Park this Saturday, knowing they will be up against a side full of confidence after Queensland’s impressive victory over the NSW Waratahs.

Tawake says the Reds’ performance has changed the outlook for this week’s clash and has pushed the Drua to lift their intensity at training.

Insert: Tawake - Focus on getting to the finals July 13 TV

With a grand final spot within reach, the Drua captain says the team is committed to giving everything on Saturday.

The Drua Women will host the Queensland Reds at Churchill Park in Lautoka at 1pm Saturday, with the winner securing a place in the Super Rugby Women grand final.

item.title: Top 8 teams confirmed for BOG
item.description: The top eight teams that will compete in this year's Extra Battle of the Giants have been confirmed following the latest round of the Extra Premier League. Extra Supermarket Labasa FC continues to lead the league standings on 28 points, holding a slim one-point lead over Dayal's Sawmillers Ba FC, which is on 27 points, while Stratum Rewa FC remains well within striking distance in third place with
item.content:encoded:
The top eight teams that will compete in this year's Extra Battle of the Giants have been confirmed following the latest round of the Extra Premier League.

Extra Supermarket Labasa FC continues to lead the league standings on 28 points, holding a slim one-point lead over Dayal's Sawmillers Ba FC, which is on 27 points, while Stratum Rewa FC remains well within striking distance in third place with 25 points.

Labasa, Ba, Rewa, Lautoka, Navua, Suva, Nadi and Nasinu have secured their position in the BOG tournament.

Nadi FC has bounced back strongly after missing out on the BiC Fiji FACT earlier this season and has now booked its place in the BOG.

Meanwhile, Nadroga FC and Tailevu/Naitasiri FC have missed out after finishing outside the top eight and will not feature in the tournament.

The Battle of the Giants pool matches will be held from 4th to 6th September, with the semi-finals and final taking place the following week at 4R Stadium, Govind Park.

With only a few rounds remaining, the battle for league honours is intensifying, with teams also looking to build momentum heading into one of Fiji's most prestigious football tournaments.

item.title: Konataci targets strong performance at Glasgow Commonwealth Games
item.description: Konataci , who is from Burerua in Tailevu, departed last Thursday ahead of the Games in Glasgow, Scotland. He is Fiji’s only para-athlete competing in the men’s discus throw at the Games.
item.content:encoded:
Konataci , who is from Burerua in Tailevu, departed last Thursday ahead of the Games in Glasgow, Scotland.

He is Fiji’s only para-athlete competing in the men’s discus throw at the Games.

Konataci will compete in the Ambulant Men's Discus Throw on Wednesday, 29th July in Glasgow.

He says preparations have been going well, although improving his throwing distance has been challenging at times.

Konataci says this will be his first Commonwealth Games and his first opportunity to represent Fiji on a major international sporting stage.

He says he is proud of the achievement and plans to focus on what he has been practising during training.

Konataci says if everything goes well, his goal is to compete for a gold medal.

The former Tailevu North College student says he competed in throwing events during secondary school and has continued to work hard despite challenges.

He is urging people in Fiji to support and pray for him as he prepares to represent the country.

The 23rd Commonwealth Games will be held in Glasgow, Scotland, from next Friday to 2nd August.

item.title: Fiji could qualify for FIFA World Cup within the next 10 years - Fiji FA
item.description: While speaking to fijivillage Sports, Fiji Football Association Chief Executive Officer Mohammed Yusuf says with 211 FIFA member associations competing for a place at the World Cup, qualification is never easy and the Fiji FA is taking a long-term approach to developing the sport from the grassroots to the elite level. He says that since 2017, Fiji has qualified for FIFA youth tournaments, giving
item.content:encoded:
While speaking to fijivillage Sports, Fiji Football Association Chief Executive Officer Mohammed Yusuf says with 211 FIFA member associations competing for a place at the World Cup, qualification is never easy and the Fiji FA is taking a long-term approach to developing the sport from the grassroots to the elite level.

He says that since 2017, Fiji has qualified for FIFA youth tournaments, giving young players valuable international experience.

Yusuf says this year, Fiji has once again earned qualification at the youth level, demonstrating that the country's football development pathway is producing results.

The CEO also stressed that one of the key initiatives is the expansion of youth football through the McDonald's League, which provides structured competition for Under-14, Under-16 and Under-19 players and the scho ols football, including the Under-18 competitions, and employers' are also helping to identify and develop talen ted players from an early age.

Yusuf stresses that off the field, Fiji Football continues to invest in infrastructure like the development work at Lomaloma Stadium and the establishment of a High Performance Centre, including a FIFA-supported artificial pitch expected to begin in stages from August to October, are designed to provide players with world-class training facilities.

Yusuf says as Fiji continues to compete in Oceania Football Confederation (OFC) competitions, hopes remain high that one day the senior national team will reach the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Quirke replaces injured Mitchell in England squad
item.description: Mitchell suffered the injury during England's 73-8 win over the FIJI Water Flying Fijians in Liverpool last Saturday. The 24-year-old Quirke recently signed with Newcastle Red Bulls after leaving Sale Sharks.
item.content:encoded:
Mitchell suffered the injury during England's 73-8 win over the FIJI Water Flying Fijians in Liverpool last Saturday.

The 24-year-old Quirke recently signed with Newcastle Red Bulls after leaving Sale Sharks.

He has made two appearances for England and became the country's youngest scrum-half in more than 90 years when he made his debut in 2021.

Quirke also played for an England XV against

a France XV in a Nations Championship warm-up match last month.

Leicester's Jack van Poortvliet and Bath's Ben Spencer are the other scrum-half options for coach Steve Borthwick ahead of England's final Test of the summer against Argentina.

Meanwhile, in other games this weekend, the Flying Fijians will take on Scotland at Murrayfield, Edingburgh at 1.10am on Sunday, South Africa will play Wales at 3.40am, and Argentina will take on England at 7.10am.

On Saturday, New Zealand will go against Ireland at 7.10pm, Japan will play France at 8.40pm, and Australia will host Italy in Perth at 10.10pm.

item.title: FIFA says no evidence ball hit overhead cable before England goal
item.description: FIFA says there is no evidence the ball touched the overhead Skycam cable before England's controversial equaliser against Norway

item.title: Labasa out to strengthen lead against Nadi as EPL title race heats up
item.description: Extra Premier League table leaders, Labasa will look to strengthen their position at the top of the standings when they take on Na
